skill.name: marketing-strategy-pmm
skill.description: Product marketing playbook for positioning, messaging, go-to-market planning, competitive intelligence, launches, market entry, sales enablement, and PMM reporting.
skill.summary: Build practical product marketing strategy, GTM plans, positioning, launch assets, and sales enablement workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown templates, marketing terminology, and checklist text in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, data exfiltration logic, or prompt injection attempts were found. One low-risk content issue remains: demo guidance could encourage use of customer data unless anonymization is required.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Findings Dismissed As Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby/shell backtick execution findings occur in Markdown fenced examples and planning templates, not executable code. The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance hits match ordinary marketing words, headings, and checklist items. No evidence found of runnable commands, shell invocation, scanning logic, credential access, or malicious intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown content blocks, slide outlines, dashboards, and templates. There is no script file, command runner, network client, or executable instruction path.

low_findings.title: Customer Data Demo Guidance Needs Privacy Guardrail
low_findings.description: The demo script advises using customer data instead of placeholder examples. This can be safe with consent and anonymization, but it could encourage accidental exposure of customer information during demos.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is direct guidance in a sales demo context, so the privacy concern is real. The risk is low because it is advisory content and does not include code, secrets, or exfiltration behavior.

content.value_statement: Product teams often need clear positioning, launch plans, and sales enablement before they can scale growth. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through PMM frameworks for ICP definition, messaging, GTM planning, competitive battlecards, and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Creates ICP definitions using firmographic, technographic, psychographic, and buyer-persona criteria.
Applies April Dunford-style positioning steps to alternatives, attributes, value, customer fit, category, and trends.
Builds launch plans with pre-launch, launch, post-launch, channel, budget, and metric guidance.
Drafts competitive battlecards with competitor overview, positioning, objection handling, and win-loss inputs.
Plans international market entry across US, Canada, UK, DACH, France, and other target regions.
Structures sales enablement assets, HubSpot reporting, launch dashboards, and quarterly PMM reviews.
limitations: Does not perform live market research or verify current competitor claims without separate browsing or supplied data.
Does not replace legal review for privacy, advertising, employment, or international market compliance.
Requires accurate product, customer, pricing, pipeline, and win-loss inputs for useful strategy recommendations.
Provides planning templates and recommendations, not automated CRM, analytics, or campaign execution.

use_cases.title: Define SaaS Positioning
use_cases.description: Turn product strengths, competitive alternatives, customer value, and target segments into clear positioning and messaging.

use_cases.title: Plan A Product Launch
use_cases.description: Create a launch tier, timeline, channel plan, enablement checklist, budget allocation, and success dashboard.

use_cases.title: Prepare Market Expansion
use_cases.description: Compare target regions, localize messaging, plan channel entry, and define market readiness requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic ICP
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a structured customer profile.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the marketing-strategy-pmm skill to create an ICP for my B2B SaaS product. Ask me for missing product, customer, deal size, geography, and churn details before drafting the profile.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Positioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the April Dunford positioning framework from this skill. Compare our product against direct competitors, indirect alternatives, and the status quo, then produce a positioning statement and messaging hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Launch Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for GTM execution planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a 90-day launch plan for a major B2B SaaS feature. Include launch tier, audience, channels, sales enablement, budget assumptions, KPIs, risks, and post-launch optimization.

prompt_templates.title: Design Competitive Enablement
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for sales and PMM collaboration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build competitive battlecards and sales enablement guidance for three competitors. Include positioning, strengths, weaknesses, objection handling, discovery questions, and win-loss feedback loops.

output_examples.input: I need a product launch plan for a new analytics dashboard aimed at mid-market SaaS companies.
output: Launch tier recommendation with rationale and target audience.
Pre-launch, launch, and post-launch timeline with owners and milestones.
Channel plan covering email, website, paid media, sales outreach, webinar, and customer marketing.
Success metrics for traffic, MQLs, SQLs, pipeline, adoption, and win rate.

output_examples.input: Help me reposition our workflow automation product against a market leader and spreadsheets.
output: Competitive alternatives mapped across direct competitor, spreadsheet workflow, and do-nothing status quo.
Unique attributes translated into customer value and business outcomes.
Recommended category choice, value proposition, proof points, and persona-specific messages.

output_examples.input: Create a battlecard for sales teams competing against Competitor A.
output: Competitor overview, target customer, pricing notes, and likely strengths.
Where we win, where they win, and how to qualify the opportunity.
Objection responses, discovery questions, proof points, and update cadence.

best_practices: Provide real customer segments, deal data, competitor names, and launch goals before asking for strategy.
Validate every recommendation with customer interviews, sales feedback, analytics, and win-loss evidence.
Anonymize customer examples and confirm permission before using customer data in demos or enablement.
anti_patterns: Using the templates as final strategy without product, market, customer, or pipeline evidence.
Copying competitor claims without verification or without documenting source quality and date.
Launching campaigns before sales, support, product, and customer success teams agree on messaging.

faq.question: What type of teams should use this skill?
faq.answer: It is best for B2B SaaS product marketing, growth, marketing leadership, and founder teams building repeatable GTM motions.

faq.question: Can it create positioning from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure positioning from customer fit, alternatives, unique attributes, value, category, and market trends.

faq.question: Does it conduct live competitor research?
faq.answer: No. It provides research frameworks and templates, but current competitor facts need supplied data or separate verified research.

faq.question: Can it help with international expansion?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes market selection, localization, channel, compliance, and budget considerations for several target regions.

faq.question: Is it suitable for sales enablement?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines battlecards, demo scripts, sales decks, one-pagers, objection handling, and reporting workflows.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Provide ICP details, customer evidence, competitors, pricing, sales cycle data, launch tier, target regions, and success metrics.
